Through its construction date and typology, the property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early

context.street_character: One-way WB | 30 km/h | Sub-standard sidewalks & roadway | E-W commercial connector

assessment.signage: Army Surplus (50% off banner in yellow and black)

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 1 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its house-form, semi-detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 0 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, the property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early.

facade_description: Single-storey facade of unspecified cladding material with a cross-gable roof, approximately 5.2 m wide, built flush to the sidewalk. The entire street frontage is occupied by a commercial storefront (Ugly Delicious Toronto). No street-facing windows are visible above the shopfront. An Army Surplus banner is also noted in the signage assessment. A pre-1889 semi-detached house-form building heavily converted for commercial use. No direct photo observation is available; description is based on database and HCD records.
